title: The Era of Real-World Human Interaction: RL from User Conversations
abstract: Introduces RLHI (Reinforcement Learning from Human Interaction), a paradigm that learns directly from in-the-wild conversations through two complementary methods: User-Guided Rewrites and User-Based Rewards. The approach links long-term user personas to turn-level preferences via persona-conditioned preference optimization.

methodology.approach: Preference learning from user interactions and long-term histories

results.instruction_following: User-Based Rewards achieves 77.9% length-controlled win rate on AlpacaEval 2.0
results.reasoning: User-Guided Rewrites raises average accuracy from 26.5 to 31.8 across four benchmarks

key_contributions: Introduction of RLHI paradigm for learning from natural human interactions
Demonstration that long-term user histories provide rich signals for personalization
Evidence that reinforcement learning outperforms supervised fine-tuning for this approach
